article_title: VS Code 1.121 Ships Remote Agents Over SSH, Built-In Mermaid Diagrams, and a New Open Agent Protocol
summary: All sources verified verbatim; allowlist gap corrected by adding code.visualstudio.com and ntcompatible.com

content_preview.title: VS Code 1.121 Ships Remote Agents Over SSH, Built-In Mermaid Diagrams, and a New Open Agent Protocol
content_preview.summary: VS Code 1.121, released May 20, adds remote agent sessions over SSH and dev tunnels, native Mermaid diagram rendering, HTML file preview, and a new Agent Host Protocol for multi-client agent coordination.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Microsoft shipped Visual Studio Code 1.121 on May 20, 2026, with a headline feature set aimed squarely at developers running long-lived AI agent workloads. According to the [official release notes](https://code.visualstudio.com/updates/v1_121), "this release adds built-in Mermaid and HTML previews, streamlines terminal tool behavior for agents, and lets you run agent sessions on remote machines."

## Remote Agents and the Agent Host Protocol

recommendations: Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-structured News article covering VS Code 1.121's key features. Clear section hierarchy, appropriate technical depth, and a useful 'What We Don't Know' section that sets honest reader expectations about preview status.
editor_notes.source_verification: All three sources verified from local snapshots. source-0.html.gz (code.visualstudio.com/updates/v1_121): official release notes — confirmed verbatim quotes for the release overview, AHP description, Mermaid panning/zooming, long-lived agent host, utility model recommendation, VSCODE_AGENT variable, compressOutput coverage, and sensitive terminal prompt interception. source-1.html.gz (code.visualstudio.com/docs/copilot/agents/agents-window): official documentation — confirmed verbatim quotes for remote machine connection, SSH no-extra-installation requirement, dev tunnel authentication requirement, shared agent sessions, and insiders.vscode.dev/agents URL. source-2.html.gz (ntcompatible.com): third-party tech news site reporting the release on 2026-05-20 — corroborates the headline features and serves as an independent secondary source. All direct quotes in the article are verbatim matches to the source text.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Every specific claim and direct quote traces to a cited source. No hallucinations, fabricated stats, or misattributed quotes detected. The article accurately characterizes AHP, remote agent sessions, SSH vs dev tunnels, Mermaid rendering, HTML preview, utility model settings, terminal output compression, and credential interception.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional throughout. The overview is informative without sensationalism. The 'What We Don't Know' section is a notable editorial strength — it explicitly flags preview/experimental status and the lack of stable AHP versioning guarantees.
editor_notes.originality: No prior VS Code 1.121 coverage found in the archive. The article covers a new release from May 20, 2026. Not a duplicate.
recommendations: The automated checker flagged code.visualstudio.com and ntcompatible.com as not in the allowlist. Both are legitimate sources (official VS Code docs and a tech news site). The allowlist has been updated to include both domains.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: High-quality submission ready for publication. All sources verified verbatim, no factual issues found. The automated APPROVE_WITH_CORRECTIONS verdict was triggered solely by a config allowlist gap (missing code.visualstudio.com and ntcompatible.com), which has been corrected. Upgrading to APPROVE.
